question: [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit / take()] Can an unprivileged taker acting as seller on a buy offer be permanently blocked from increasing their own credit in a gated market when the enterGate contract internally calls an oracle that reverts under realistic price-feed conditions, under preconditions: market has enterGate != address(0), buyer == offer.maker, buyerCreditIncrease > 0, oracle used by enterGate is the same oracle used for collateral valuation and is temporarily reverting, triggering call sequence: taker calls take() -> Midnight evaluates buyerCreditIncrease > 0 -> Midnight calls IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(buyer) -> gate's internal oracle call reverts -> take() reverts with BuyerGatedFromIncreasingCredit, violating invariant: enter_gate_must_not_block_exit and users_can_exit_even_if_enter_gate_reverts when the buyer is trying to reduce existing debt (buyerCreditIncrease > 0 only because units > buyerPos.debt), causing scoped impact: fund freeze — valid credit-increasing take that reduces debt is blocked for the duration of oracle unavailability? Proof idea: deploy oracle-dependent enterGate, set oracle to revert, fuzz units near buyerPos.debt boundary and assert take() reverts even when buyer has outstanding debt to reduce.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseDebt / take()] Can an unprivileged maker who published a sell offer be blocked from having their debt increased by a taker when the enterGate.canIncreaseDebt(seller) call reverts because the gate contract reads a token balance via a token that has been paused or blacklisted, under preconditions: offer.buy == false so seller == offer.maker, sellerDebtIncrease > 0, enterGate is a token-balance-gated contract, loan token or governance token is paused, triggering call sequence: taker calls take() -> sellerDebtIncrease computed > 0 -> IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseDebt(seller) called -> gate calls token.balanceOf(seller) -> token reverts -> take() reverts with SellerGatedFromIncreasingDebt, violating invariant: gate checks must apply to all entry paths but must not block valid protocol-level actions when gate's own dependency fails, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — maker's sell offer becomes untakeable for all takers while token is paused? Proof idea: deploy token-balance enterGate, pause the token, attempt take() and assert revert; unpause and assert success.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate / liquidate()] Can an unprivileged liquidator be blocked from liquidating a genuinely unhealthy borrower when the liquidatorGate contract reverts due to an internal state inconsistency (e.g., a mapping lookup on a deleted or uninitialized key), under preconditions: market.liquidatorGate != address(0), borrower is unhealthy (debt > maxDebt), liquidatorGate.canLiquidate(msg.sender) reverts instead of returning false, triggering call sequence: liquidator calls liquidate() -> Midnight checks ILiquidatorGate(liquidatorGate).canLiquidate(msg.sender) -> gate reverts -> liquidate() reverts with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating, violating invariant: unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation failure — unhealthy position cannot be liquidated, bad debt accumulates, protocol solvency degrades? Proof idea: deploy reverting liquidatorGate, create unhealthy position, call liquidate() and assert revert; fix gate and assert liquidation succeeds.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit / take()] Can a valid post-maturity credit-increasing take (buyer reducing existing debt by buying units) be incorrectly blocked by an enterGate that reverts when called after market.maturity, under preconditions: block.timestamp > market.maturity, offer.buy == true, buyer == offer.maker, buyerCreditIncrease > 0 (buyer has no existing debt to net against), enterGate is time-aware and reverts for post-maturity timestamps, triggering call sequence: taker calls take() post-maturity -> CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity check passes (sellerDebtIncrease == 0) -> buyerCreditIncrease > 0 -> IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(buyer) called -> gate reverts -> take() reverts, violating invariant: post-maturity unwinding must not be blocked by enter gate, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — lenders cannot acquire credit post-maturity to unwind positions? Proof idea: deploy time-gated enterGate that reverts post-maturity, warp to maturity+1, attempt credit-increasing take and assert revert.

[File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: ILiquidatorGate.canLiquidate / liquidate()] Can a zero-asset bad-debt-realization liquidation (seizedAssets == 0, repaidUnits == 0) be blocked by a liquidatorGate that reverts or returns false for the calling liquidator, under preconditions: market.liquidatorGate != address(0), borrower has bad debt (all collateral value < debt), liquidator calls liquidate() with seizedAssets == 0 and repaidUnits == 0 to realize bad debt, liquidatorGate.canLiquidate(msg.sender) returns false, triggering call sequence: liquidator calls liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) -> gate check fails -> revert with LiquidatorGatedFromLiquidating, violating invariant: bad debt must reduce lender credit exactly once and proportionally; bad debt realization must not be permanently blocked, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption — bad debt is never socialized, lender credit remains inflated above actual recoverable value? [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit / take()] Can an authorized operator calling take() on behalf of a taker be incorrectly blocked when the enterGate is designed to check the taker's address but the gate's internal logic mistakenly checks msg.sender (which is Midnight, not the taker), under preconditions: isAuthorized[taker][operator] == true, operator calls take() with taker parameter set to taker's address, enterGate.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) is called where buyer == taker, but gate internally uses msg.sender (== Midnight) for its allowlist check, triggering call sequence: operator calls take() -> Midnight calls IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(buyer) with buyer == taker -> gate checks msg.sender == Midnight against its allowlist -> Midnight is not in allowlist -> gate returns false -> take() reverts, violating invariant: authorization must only allow intended account delegation; gate checks must apply correctly to the actual account, causing scoped impact: gate bypass or fund freeze depending on gate design — authorized operator flow is blocked or gate is trivially bypassed? [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit / take()] Can a taker be blocked from taking a buy offer (where offer.buy == true and buyer == offer.maker) when the enterGate checks the buyer's (maker's) eligibility but the maker is a smart contract that has been selfdestructed or has no code, causing the gate's delegatecall or staticcall to the maker to revert, under preconditions: offer.buy == true, offer.maker is a contract address that has been selfdestructed, enterGate calls maker's address for some check, buyerCreditIncrease > 0, triggering call sequence: taker calls take() -> IEnterGate(enterGate).canIncreaseCredit(offer.maker) -> gate calls offer.maker -> revert -> take() reverts, violating invariant: gate checks must not block valid taker actions due to maker's external state, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — all takes against a maker whose contract was selfdestructed are permanently blocked? [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit + canIncreaseDebt / take()] Can a take() that involves both a credit increase for the buyer and a debt increase for the seller be blocked when the enterGate.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) returns true but enterGate.canIncreaseDebt(seller) reverts due to a state change in the gate between the two calls (e.g., a time-based gate that crosses a boundary between the two external calls), under preconditions: enterGate is time-sensitive, buyerCreditIncrease > 0, sellerDebtIncrease > 0, gate state changes between the two calls within the same transaction, triggering call sequence: take() -> canIncreaseCredit(buyer) returns true -> [gate state changes] -> canIncreaseDebt(seller) reverts -> take() reverts, violating invariant: gate checks must be consistent within a single take() execution, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — valid takes are blocked due to gate state inconsistency within a single transaction? [File: 'src/interfaces/IGate.sol -> Scope: Medium Incorrect revert behavior that blocks valid user actions under realistic oracle, token, callback, or authorization conditions'] [Function: IEnterGate.canIncreaseCredit / take()] Can a buyer's credit increase be incorrectly blocked when buyerCreditIncrease is computed as a small positive value due to rounding (e.g., 1 wei) even though the economic intent is a pure debt reduction, under preconditions: buyer has existing debt, units is chosen such that units > buyerPos.debt by exactly 1 wei, buyerCreditIncrease == 1, enterGate.canIncreaseCredit(buyer) returns false for this buyer, triggering call sequence: taker calls take() with units == buyerPos.debt + 1 -> buyerCreditIncrease == 1 -> gate check fails -> revert, but take() with units == buyerPos.debt succeeds without gate check, violating invariant: rounding must not cause gate checks to block economically equivalent actions, causing scoped impact: fund freeze — buyer cannot fully close debt position in a single take due to 1-wei rounding triggering gate?
